name: Adam on earth, part 1
epithet: He forgot, he slipped, and then he turned back

era.anchor: At the very start of us

title: Adam on earth, part 1: the first slip, the first return
meta_description: Day 3 of 29: Iblis blames Adam instead of his own sin, the covenant every soul made in the loins of Adam, why the prophet forgot and slipped, and the road back to Allah. Retold faithfully from Mufti Ismail Menk's Stories of the Prophets.
keywords: Adam on earth, Mufti Menk, stories of the prophets, Iblis, the covenant of Adam, alastu bi rabbikum, tawbah, repentance, witch doctors, jinn, Buruja

video.title: Stories Of The Prophets-03~Aadam on Earth (Part 1)

source_note: Retold faithfully from Mufti Ismail Menk's Stories of the Prophets, episode 3 (Adam, peace be upon him, on earth, part 1). Qur'an: Sahih International, verified via quran.ai. The narration is Mufti Menk's, the phrasing is Buruja's.
lede: Yesterday Allah shaped the first human being with His own hand. Tonight the story turns to a single, unglamorous thing that happened next, and that the whole rest of human history is built on: he forgot, he slipped, and then he turned back. The first sin in creation belonged to Iblis, who would not bow. The first repentance belonged to Adam, who fell and came home. We are the children of the one who returned.

This is day three of twenty-nine, still in the opening chapter, the first human beings, retold faithfully from Mufti Ismail Menk's series. He picks up loose threads from last night, and out of them draws something startlingly close: the trick Iblis pulled on Adam, peace be upon him, is the exact trick he still pulls on you.

sections.h2: The first sin, and who really owned it

ayah.arabic: فَقُلْنَا يَٰٓـَٔادَمُ إِنَّ هَٰذَا عَدُوٌّ لَّكَ وَلِزَوْجِكَ فَلَا يُخْرِجَنَّكُمَا مِنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ فَتَشْقَىٰٓ
ayah.text: So We said, "O Adam, indeed this is an enemy to you and to your wife. Then let him not remove you from Paradise so you would suffer.

paras: Go back to the moment the angels were told to prostrate to Adam, and one creature refused. Not bowed late, not bowed grudgingly: refused, flat. I am better than him, he said, and with that he became the accursed, the one thrown out. Mufti Menk stops to name it for what it is. That was the first sin ever committed against Allah in all of creation, and it belonged to Iblis. Allah warned the new couple plainly: O Adam, this one is an enemy to you and to your wife, do not let him drive you out of the garden into hardship.

Then watch what the enemy does once he is expelled, because it is the most human thing in the world. He does not say, my Lord, I was wrong, I sinned, forgive me. He turns and blames Adam: because of him I was thrown out. But was Adam the reason Iblis was cursed? No. His own arrogance was. He had a problem and he refused to look at its root, so he could never solve it. Here Mufti Menk turns the mirror on us, and it stings: when something breaks in our lives, at home, at work, between whole nations, how often do we point everywhere except at the cause we are sitting on top of? People look at the Muslim and cry terrorist and never once ask how a thing began, what oppression lit it, where it went wrong. Islam preaches none of it: peace, dignity, security, harmony, in every direction. But the criminal who makes havoc and then blames the victim is only doing again what Iblis did first.

sections.h2: The enemy you never see coming
paras: Iblis told Allah he would come at this human being from every angle, from the front and the behind, from the right and the left. Mufti Menk points out the one direction he left off the list: he never said from above. He will not come from above, because from above comes the help of Allah, and Iblis has no reach there. That is the whole game. A thief who announces himself gets nothing; you would simply leave. So he never announces himself. When you look one way he slips in from the other, and he works the way wireless works, a signal you cannot see crossing from somewhere to here. That is how he reached Adam in the garden after he had already been thrown out of it: not marching in as himself, but hidden, in disguise, in one report through the throat of a serpent that carried him past the gate. Allah knows exactly how. What the story fixes is the manner: he came in secret, never out in the open.

And he came selling two things people will always buy: a tree that, he whispered, would give Adam life that never ends and a kingdom that never runs out. Health and wealth, forever. Mufti Menk lets you feel how old and how current that pitch is. Watch what happens, he says, when one of us falls ill. We pray, we go to the doctor, we take the medicine, a month passes, two months, no cure yet. And right there the same whisper returns: there is another way, let me show you, come to the fortune teller, the witch doctor, the one who calls himself a great healer and will name who put magic on you. Same bait Iblis used on the first man, aimed now at his children. The lesson is not that we abandon medicine. It is that we will not chase health, or wealth, through anything that displeases Allah, because we have to die either way, and to die having failed the test is the only real loss.

ayah.arabic: إِنَّ عِبَادِى لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَٰنٌ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ ٱلْغَاوِينَ
ayah.text: Indeed, My servants - no authority will you have over them, except those who follow you of the deviators.

ayah.arabic: وَأَنَّهُۥ كَانَ رِجَالٌ مِّنَ ٱلْإِنسِ يَعُوذُونَ بِرِجَالٍ مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ فَزَادُوهُمْ رَهَقًا
ayah.text: And there were men from mankind who sought refuge in men from the jinn, so they [only] increased them in burden [i.e., sin].

paras: This is the section Mufti Menk lingers on, because he has watched it ruin people. The healer tells you to cut a precise number of lemons, weigh each one, squeeze out so many drops, add a little saffron, arrange bones in a certain shape. The instructions get sillier the deeper you go: bring a tongue, slaughter a dove, sometimes worse. And the whole time, he says, Iblis is laughing, at us and at Allah, because this is exactly what he promised. Didn't I tell You I would lead them to worship me? Tell them to pray when they are sick, tell them to seek proper treatment, and they will not listen. Tell them to cut eighty lemons and they will do it. He once heard of a man who told a woman that someone in her own family had put magic on her, and it shattered the whole family. How did he know? No angel told him. If Jibril did not come, then Iblis did. And when Iblis comes he plays Muslim: I am a clean jinn, a believing jinn, I knew the Prophet. Do not be fooled.

Allah draws the line that protects you. Over My true servants, He tells Iblis, you have no authority at all, only over those who choose to follow you. And about those who run to the jinn for help, Allah is blunt: men who sought refuge in jinn were only increased in burden, more lost, more wretched, not less. So no, we are not permitted to seek the aid of the jinn, and the ones who boast that they own a jinn or command one are the most fooled of all. Think about it, Mufti Menk says: Iblis deceived Adam, who had seen him, who knew exactly what he was, face to face. How much more easily will he deceive us, who have never even laid eyes on him? There is no quick fix. Wealth comes by honest work, slow and sure and lawful, or by robbery, fast and cursed and lived in fear. Health comes by prayer and the Qur'an and lawful medicine, slowly, surely, or by the overnight cure of the witch doctor, who is only Iblis, laughing.

sections.h2: Why did Adam slip at all

ayah.arabic: وَلَقَدْ عَهِدْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ ءَادَمَ مِن قَبْلُ فَنَسِىَ وَلَمْ نَجِدْ لَهُۥ عَزْمًا
ayah.text: And We had already taken a promise from Adam before, but he forgot; and We found not in him determination.

paras: People come to Mufti Menk and ask the question that sounds so reasonable: a prophet, the first man, why would he sin? And he answers it, but then he flips it like a teacher who sees the trap. Listen to the verse: We made a covenant with Adam before, but he forgot, and We found in him no firm resolve. He did not plot it. He was not determined to disobey. He forgot, and he slipped. And the Arabic almost confesses it: one reading of the word insan, the word for a human being, ties it to nisyan, forgetting, as though to be human is to be the one who forgets. So yes, Adam forgot. But here is the flip: why are you sinning? You do not forget. You plan it, you calculate it, you carry it out, you enjoy it afterward, and then you plan it again. The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ gave the cure in a single line: glad tidings of Paradise to the one whose own faults keep him too busy to inspect the faults of everyone else. Iblis would love nothing more than to keep you absorbed in why Adam slipped while you walk calmly into a sin you fully intend.

sections.h2: The promise every soul has already made

ayah.arabic: وَإِذْ أَخَذَ رَبُّكَ مِنۢ بَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ مِن ظُهُورِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ وَأَشْهَدَهُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ ۛ شَهِدْنَآ ۛ أَن تَقُولُوا۟ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَٰمَةِ إِنَّا كُنَّا عَنْ هَٰذَا غَٰفِلِينَ
ayah.text: And [mention] when your Lord took from the children of Adam - from their loins - their descendants and made them testify of themselves, [saying to them], "Am I not your Lord?" They said, "Yes, we have testified." [This] - lest you should say on the Day of Resurrection, "Indeed, we were of this unaware."

ayah.arabic: أَوْ تَقُولُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَشْرَكَ ءَابَآؤُنَا مِن قَبْلُ وَكُنَّا ذُرِّيَّةً مِّنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ ۖ أَفَتُهْلِكُنَا بِمَا فَعَلَ ٱلْمُبْطِلُونَ
ayah.text: Or [lest] you say, "It was only that our fathers associated [others in worship] with Allāh before, and we were but descendants after them. Then would You destroy us for what the falsifiers have done?"

paras: Here is the moment Mufti Menk says we have all forgotten, and the Qur'an says so on purpose. When Adam was created, Allah drew out from his back every single soul that would ever descend from him, all of us, to the last person before the Hour, and showed them to him. Then He put one question to every soul at once: am I not your Lord? And every soul answered, yes, we testify. Allah seals the scene with the reason He records it: so that you cannot stand on the Day of Resurrection and say you knew nothing, and cannot say you were only following the shirk of your forefathers, helpless children born after them. The excuse is closed before it is made. Each soul gave its own word.

Ask yourself honestly, Mufti Menk says, do you remember that day? Not one of us does. So Allah, in His mercy, says: no matter, I will keep sending you reminders. That is what the Qur'an is. The covenant in your chest, the faint pull toward your Lord you have felt your whole life, has a date older than the world, and revelation comes to wake it. Which means belief is not something foreign being asked of you. It is the oldest promise you ever made, being called in. And the warning folded inside it is real: no one will be judged through a forefather. Each of us will stand before Allah with no translator between, answer for ourselves alone, and the only safe answer is the one we keep ready now.

sections.h2: Turning the page, the road back

ayah.arabic: قَالَ ٱهْبِطَا مِنْهَا جَمِيعًۢا ۖ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ ۖ فَإِمَّا يَأْتِيَنَّكُم مِّنِّى هُدًى فَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ هُدَاىَ فَلَا يَضِلُّ وَلَا يَشْقَىٰ
ayah.text: [Allāh] said, "Descend from it [i.e., Paradise] - all, [your descendants] being enemies to one another. And if there should come to you guidance from Me - then whoever follows My guidance will neither go astray [in the world] nor suffer [in the Hereafter].

ayah.arabic: وَمَنْ أَعْرَضَ عَن ذِكْرِى فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ مَعِيشَةً ضَنكًا وَنَحْشُرُهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَٰمَةِ أَعْمَىٰ
ayah.text: And whoever turns away from My remembrance - indeed, he will have a depressed [i.e., difficult] life, and We will gather [i.e., raise] him on the Day of Resurrection blind."

paras: When Adam was sent down to the earth, Allah did not abandon him. He gave him the promise that holds the entire human story together: I will keep sending you guidance, and whoever follows My guidance will not go astray and will not be wretched. But whoever turns away from My remembrance, his life will be narrow, pinched, joyless however full his hands are, and he will be raised blind on the Day of Resurrection. This, Mufti Menk says, is why the restless and the discontented are restless: not for lack of money, but for distance from Allah. The atheist, the agnostic, the one sure this life is all there is, can be drowning in things and still find something missing, because a human being was not built to live without his Lord.

And underneath every line of it runs the mercy that is the real heart of tonight. Mufti Menk says it almost gently: Allah's mercy is so vast that a man could follow Iblis for seventy years, and if for one second he turns back to Allah, every drop of that evil is wiped away and he begins on a clean page. Turning the page is not hard. But a page does not turn itself; you have to put your finger to it and move it. Adam did. In one narration he stands before his Lord and asks, my Lord, did You not create me with Your hand, honour me, have the angels bow to me? Yes. Then if I obey You and ask Your forgiveness, will You return me to the garden? And Allah says, yes. That is the whole message, and it is addressed to you: the first human being fell, repented, and was promised home. The door he walked back through has never once been shut behind him.

dua.arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَآ أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَٰسِرِينَ
dua.translit: Rabbana zalamna anfusana wa in lam taghfir lana wa tarhamna lanakunanna minal-khasirin
dua.en: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers. (the words of Adam and Hawwa, Surah al-A'raf 7:23)

lessons_intro: Adam's first day on earth is a lesson in returning, drawn straight from the points of benefit Mufti Menk pulls out of the story.

lessons.bold: Look at the root, not the scapegoat.
lessons.text: Iblis blamed Adam for an expulsion his own arrogance earned. When something breaks in your life, find the cause you are sitting on before you point at anyone else.
The enemy works in disguise, from every side but the one where Allah's help waits. Raise your hands to the Owner of the universe and the devil has no road to you.

Chasing health or wealth through the witch doctor, the fortune teller, the jinn, is the oldest trap there is. Over Allah's true servants the devil has no authority. Pray, seek lawful means, and refuse the overnight cure.
Adam forgot, with no firm resolve to disobey. We plan our sins and then ask why a prophet slipped. Glad tidings to the one whose own faults keep him too busy to count everyone else's.
Follow Iblis seventy years, turn to Allah for one second in truth, and it is wiped clean. But the page does not turn itself. Put your finger to it and move it tonight.

faq.q: What was the very first sin, and was it Adam's?
faq.a: No. The first sin in creation was Iblis refusing to prostrate to Adam out of arrogance, saying he was better, after which Allah warned Adam, 'this is an enemy to you and to your wife' (Surah Ta-Ha 20:117). Adam's slip came later and was different in kind: he forgot, with no firm resolve to disobey (Surah Ta-Ha 20:115). The first sin was pride; the first repentance was Adam's return.

faq.q: What is the covenant Allah took from the children of Adam?
faq.a: When Adam was created, Allah brought forth from his loins every soul that would ever descend from him and made each one testify, 'Am I not your Lord?' Every soul answered, 'Yes, we testify' (Surah al-A'raf 7:172-173). Allah records it so that no one can claim on the Day of Resurrection that they were unaware, or that they were only following their forefathers. Mufti Menk notes that none of us remembers that day, which is exactly why Allah keeps sending reminders, the Qur'an among them.

faq.q: Why does Mufti Menk warn so strongly against witch doctors and 'owning' jinn?
faq.a: Because it is Iblis's oldest trick, the same bait of health and wealth he used on Adam, now aimed at Adam's children. Allah says those who sought refuge in jinn were only increased in burden and misguidance (Surah al-Jinn 72:6), and that over His true servants the devil has no authority (Surah al-Hijr 15:42). Seeking the aid of jinn is not permitted; the cures come dressed as quick fixes and lead away from Allah. Pray, use lawful medicine and the Qur'an, and refuse anything that displeases Allah.

faq.q: How does this connect to my own faith today?
faq.a: Directly. The covenant 'Am I not your Lord' means belief is not something foreign being demanded of you; it is the oldest promise you ever made, being called in. And Adam's return is the model for your tawbah: he fell, repented, and was promised home, and the same door stands open for you. As Mufti Menk puts it, follow Iblis for seventy years and turn back for one sincere second, and it is all wiped clean.

faq.q: How does Adam's story connect to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ?
faq.a: The chain of guidance Allah promised Adam, 'whoever follows My guidance will not go astray' (Surah Ta-Ha 20:123), runs through every prophet and completes in the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, the final reminder Allah sent to wake the covenant in every chest. And the cure for our habit of inspecting others' faults comes from him ﷺ: glad tidings to the one whose own faults keep him busy from the faults of people.

closing_heading: Why this day stays with you
closing: The first day on earth could have been remembered for the loss: the garden behind him, the long exile ahead, an enemy already at work. Instead the Qur'an remembers it for the return. Iblis sinned and blamed everyone but himself, and stayed cursed. Adam forgot, slipped, owned it, asked, and was promised the way back. Between those two responses lies the whole of your life, and the story of Adam's first day is Allah quietly showing you which one leads home.

So do the one thing Iblis never could. Put your finger to the page and turn it. O Allah, You who took our promise before we were born and never stopped sending us back to it, wake the covenant in our chests, keep the devil from every side of us, and let us answer Your call the way our father Adam did, not with excuses, but with rabbana zalamna anfusana, our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, forgive us and have mercy on us. Return us, as You returned him, to the home You prepared. Ameen.

slides:
Day 3 of 29.

The first sin in creation was not Adam's.

It was Iblis, who would not bow, and then blamed Adam for the expulsion his own arrogance earned.

Iblis said he would come at you from the front, the back, the right, the left.

He never said from above.

Because from above comes the help of Allah, and there he has no reach.

Watch what happens when one of us falls ill.

We pray. We see the doctor. Two months pass, no cure.

And the whisper returns: there's another way. Come to the healer.

Same bait Iblis sold Adam. Health and wealth, forever.

Cut these lemons. Weigh each one. Bring a tongue. Slaughter a dove.

The whole time, Iblis is laughing.

Didn't I tell You I'd lead them to worship me? Tell them to pray, they won't. Tell them to cut eighty lemons, they will.

A prophet, the first man, why would he sin?

He forgot. No firm resolve to disobey.

But you? You don't forget. You plan it, enjoy it, and plan it again.

Watch how the question itself is a trap.

Before you were born, Allah drew every soul from the back of Adam and asked one question:

Am I not your Lord?

And every soul, yours included, answered: yes, we testify.

You don't remember that day. None of us does.

So Allah says: no matter, I'll keep sending you reminders.

That is what the Qur'an is. Belief is the oldest promise you ever made, being called in.

Follow Iblis for seventy years.

Turn back to Allah for one sincere second, and every drop of it is wiped clean.

Turning the page is not hard. But a page does not turn itself.

Adam put his finger to it. So can you.
social.caption: Day 3 of 29: Adam on earth, part 1. The first sin belonged to Iblis, who would not bow and then blamed everyone but himself. The first repentance belonged to Adam, who forgot, slipped, and turned back. In between sits the covenant every soul already made, 'Am I not your Lord?', and the trick the devil still runs on all of us. Retold faithfully from Mufti Ismail Menk's beloved series (the full episode is embedded on the site), Qur'an verified via quran.ai. A prophet a day, connected back to the Qur'an and the life of the Prophet ﷺ, at buruja.com. #StoriesOfTheProphets #MuftiMenk #Adam #tawbah #Quran #Buruja #islam #repentance

email.subject: The first sin was not Adam's
email.preview: Day 3 of 29: the first slip, the first return, and a promise you've already made.
email.body:
Assalamu alaykum,

Yesterday Allah shaped the first human being with His own hand. Today the story turns to the unglamorous thing that happened next, the thing the whole rest of human history is built on: Adam forgot, he slipped, and then he turned back.

The first sin in all creation was not his. It belonged to Iblis, who refused to bow, and who then did the most human thing imaginable: he blamed Adam for an expulsion his own arrogance had earned. Mufti Menk turns that mirror on us, because we do it constantly, pointing everywhere except at the cause we are sitting on top of.

And the trick Iblis pulled on Adam is the exact one he still pulls on you. He promised a tree of endless life and endless wealth, health and riches forever, and Mufti Menk shows how old and how current that pitch is: we fall ill, we pray, we take the medicine, and when the cure is slow the same whisper returns, there is another way, come to the witch doctor, the fortune teller, the one who will name who put magic on you. Cut these lemons, bring this, slaughter that. The whole time, he says, Iblis is laughing, at us and at Allah, because this is exactly what he promised to do.

But the heart of today is mercy. Before you were born, Allah drew every soul out of the back of Adam and asked one question, 'Am I not your Lord?', and every soul, yours included, answered yes (Surah al-A'raf 7:172). You do not remember it. So Allah keeps sending reminders, and the Qur'an is one. Belief is not something foreign being asked of you. It is the oldest promise you ever made, being called in.

And the road back is open. Follow Iblis seventy years, Mufti Menk says, and turn to Allah for one sincere second, and every drop of it is wiped clean. The page does not turn itself, but Adam put his finger to it and was promised home. So can you.

Read day 3, and watch Mufti Menk's full lecture, at the link below.
